title: podman diff results are not showing all differences when build from a readonly image
body:
### Issue Description

With `additionalimagestore` configured, I use root podman pulling a image to the additional image store, the image should be read-only for the rootless user. Then I use rootless podman to build a image based the read-only image, then use `podman diff` to list the differences between the new built image and the read-only image, the diff results are not correct.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE        R/O
docker.io/library/alpine  latest      7e01a0d0a1dc  2 weeks ago  7.63 MB     true

$ cat dockerfile3
FROM alpine
COPY filea /filea
COPY fileb /fileb
RUN rm /filea

$ podman build -f dockerfile3 -t difftest
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine
STEP 2/4: COPY filea /filea
--> 286e3df1f2d
STEP 3/4: COPY fileb /fileb
--> 51f4d8afb0f
STEP 4/4: RUN rm /filea
COMMIT difftest
--> 0550c5c8b36
Successfully tagged localhost/difftest:latest
0550c5c8b36c16a3f7b5ee3463b32e6a29ea196a2d63272fb2dde1cb76c1652c

$ podman image tree difftest
Image ID: 0550c5c8b36c
Tags:     [localhost/difftest:latest]
Size:     7.637MB
Image Layers
├── ID: 4693057ce236 Size: 7.626MB Top Layer of: [docker.io/library/alpine:latest]
├── ID: 880ac05750c7 Size: 1.536kB
├── ID: d982c9525cb7 Size: 1.536kB
└── ID: 9eefa2d1afca Size:  5.12kB Top Layer of: [localhost/difftest:latest]

$ podman diff 880ac05750c7
A /filea

$ podman diff d982c9525cb7
A /fileb

$ podman diff 9eefa2d1afca
C /etc
D /filea

$ podman diff difftest alpine
C /etc
D /filea
```


### Describe the results you received

The last diff result between the `alpine` image layer and `difftest` is incorrect, it only show the delete for `filea`.

### Describe the results you expected

The last diff result between the `alpine` image layer and `difftest` is incorrect, the result should have only included the add for `fileb`, but not delete for `filea`.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
